title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 1
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {79} \quad M_{A1B1} = {11.29}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {3.43} \quad n_{A1B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {120} \quad M_{A1B2} = {17.14}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {6.86} \quad n_{A1B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {93} \quad M_{A1B3} = {13.29}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {5.43} \quad n_{A1B3} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {80} \quad M_{A2B1} = {11.43}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {9.71} \quad n_{A2B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {103} \quad M_{A2B2} = {14.71}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {5.43} \quad n_{A2B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {110} \quad M_{A2B3} = {15.71}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {3.43} \quad n_{A2B3} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {121} \quad M_{A3B1} = {17.29}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {5.43} \quad n_{A3B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {78} \quad M_{A3B2} = {11.14}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {2.86} \quad n_{A3B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {118} \quad M_{A3B3} = {16.86}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {0.86} \quad n_{A3B3} = {7}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {63} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {62}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {63} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {54}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13336} - \frac{902^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13336} - \frac{813604}{63}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13336} - {12914.35}\)  \(SS_{total} = {421.65}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {3.43 + 6.86 + 5.43 + 9.71 + 5.43 + 3.43 + 5.43 + 2.86 + 0.86}\)  \(SS_{within} = {43.44}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {421.65} - {43.44}\)  \(SS_{between} = {378.21}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {292}  \quad n_{A_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {293}  \quad n_{A_2} = {21}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {317}  \quad n_{A_3} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.02}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{292}^2}{21} + \frac{{293}^2}{21} + \frac{{317}^2}{21}} - \frac{{902}^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{85264}}{21} + \frac{{85849}}{21} + \frac{{100489}}{21}} - \frac{{813604}}{63}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{4060.19} + {4088.05} + {4785.19}} - {12914.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {12933.43} - {12914.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {19.08}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{19.08}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {9.54}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{9.54}{0.8}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {11.92}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{19.08}{{421.65} - {40.02} - {319.11}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{19.08}{62.52}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {11.92}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {280}  \quad n_{B_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {301}  \quad n_{B_2} = {21}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {321}  \quad n_{B_3} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.02}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{280}^2}{21} + \frac{{301}^2}{21} + \frac{{321}^2}{21}} - \frac{{902}^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{78400}}{21} + \frac{{90601}}{21} + \frac{{103041}}{21}} - \frac{{813604}}{63}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{3733.33} + {4314.33} + {4906.71}} - {12914.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {12954.37} - {12914.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {40.02}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{40.02}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {20.01}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{20.01}{0.8}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {25.01}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{40.02}{{421.65} - {19.08} - {319.11}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{40.02}{83.46}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.48}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {25.01}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.48}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.69}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {378.21} - {19.08} - {40.02}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {319.11}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{319.11}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {79.78}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{79.78}{0.8}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {99.72}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{319.11}{{421.65} - {19.08} - {40.02}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{319.11}{362.55}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.88}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {54}) = {99.72}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.88}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 2
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {61} \quad M_{A1B1} = {10.17}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {16.83} \quad n_{A1B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {68} \quad M_{A1B2} = {11.33}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {23.33} \quad n_{A1B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {99} \quad M_{A1B3} = {16.5}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {45.5} \quad n_{A1B3} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {75} \quad M_{A2B1} = {12.5}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {45.5} \quad n_{A2B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {107} \quad M_{A2B2} = {17.83}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {84.83} \quad n_{A2B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {78} \quad M_{A2B3} = {13.0}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {36.0} \quad n_{A2B3} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {75} \quad M_{A3B1} = {12.5}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {53.5} \quad n_{A3B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {60} \quad M_{A3B2} = {10.0}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {40.0} \quad n_{A3B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {77} \quad M_{A3B3} = {12.83}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {28.83} \quad n_{A3B3} = {6}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {54} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {53}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {54} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {45}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {9784} - \frac{700^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {9784} - \frac{490000}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {9784} - {9074.07}\)  \(SS_{total} = {709.93}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {16.83 + 23.33 + 45.5 + 45.5 + 84.83 + 36.0 + 53.5 + 40.0 + 28.83}\)  \(SS_{within} = {374.32}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {709.93} - {374.32}\)  \(SS_{between} = {335.61}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {228}  \quad n_{A_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {260}  \quad n_{A_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {212}  \quad n_{A_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{228}^2}{18} + \frac{{260}^2}{18} + \frac{{212}^2}{18}} - \frac{{700}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{51984}}{18} + \frac{{67600}}{18} + \frac{{44944}}{18}} - \frac{{490000}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{2888.0} + {3755.56} + {2496.89}} - {9074.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {9140.45} - {9074.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {66.38}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{66.38}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {33.19}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{33.19}{8.32}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {3.99}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{66.38}{{709.93} - {51.6} - {217.63}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{66.38}{440.7}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.15}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {45}) = {3.99}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.15}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {211}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {235}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {254}  \quad n_{B_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{211}^2}{18} + \frac{{235}^2}{18} + \frac{{254}^2}{18}} - \frac{{700}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{44521}}{18} + \frac{{55225}}{18} + \frac{{64516}}{18}} - \frac{{490000}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{2473.39} + {3068.06} + {3584.22}} - {9074.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {9125.67} - {9074.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {51.6}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{51.6}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {25.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{25.8}{8.32}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {3.1}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{51.6}{{709.93} - {66.38} - {217.63}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{51.6}{425.92}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.12}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {45}) = {3.1}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.12}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.77}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {335.61} - {66.38} - {51.6}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {217.63}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{217.63}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {54.41}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{54.41}{8.32}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {6.54}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{217.63}{{709.93} - {66.38} - {51.6}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{217.63}{591.95}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.37}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {45}) = {6.54}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.37}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 3
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {599} \quad M_{A1B1} = {49.92}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {1364.92} \quad n_{A1B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {758} \quad M_{A1B2} = {63.17}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {1635.67} \quad n_{A1B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {626} \quad M_{A1B3} = {52.17}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {1665.67} \quad n_{A1B3} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {799} \quad M_{A2B1} = {66.58}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {2248.92} \quad n_{A2B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {598} \quad M_{A2B2} = {49.83}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {2489.67} \quad n_{A2B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {649} \quad M_{A2B3} = {54.08}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {1176.92} \quad n_{A2B3} = {12}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {72} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {71}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {72} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {66}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {239119} - \frac{4029^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {239119} - \frac{16232841}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {239119} - {225456.12}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13662.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1364.92 + 1635.67 + 1665.67 + 2248.92 + 2489.67 + 1176.92}\)  \(SS_{within} = {10581.77}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {13662.88} - {10581.77}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3081.11}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1983}  \quad n_{A_1} = {36}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {2046}  \quad n_{A_2} = {36}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.04}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1983}^2}{36} + \frac{{2046}^2}{36}} - \frac{{4029}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{3932289}}{36} + \frac{{4186116}}{36}} - \frac{{16232841}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{109230.25} + {116281.0}} - {225456.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {225511.25} - {225456.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {55.13}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{55.13}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {55.13}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{55.13}{160.33}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.34}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{55.13}{{13662.88} - {325.76} - {2700.22}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{55.13}{10636.9}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {66}) = {0.34}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1398}  \quad n_{B_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1356}  \quad n_{B_2} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {1275}  \quad n_{B_3} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.94}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1398}^2}{24} + \frac{{1356}^2}{24} + \frac{{1275}^2}{24}} - \frac{{4029}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1954404}}{24} + \frac{{1838736}}{24} + \frac{{1625625}}{24}} - \frac{{16232841}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{81433.5} + {76614.0} + {67734.38}} - {225456.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {225781.88} - {225456.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {325.76}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{325.76}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {162.88}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{162.88}{160.33}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {1.02}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{325.76}{{13662.88} - {55.13} - {2700.22}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{325.76}{10907.53}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {1.02}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.94}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {3081.11} - {55.13} - {325.76}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {2700.22}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{2700.22}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {1350.11}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{1350.11}{160.33}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {8.42}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2700.22}{{13662.88} - {55.13} - {325.76}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2700.22}{13281.99}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.2}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {8.42}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.2}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 4
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {226} \quad M_{A1B1} = {45.2}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {288.8} \quad n_{A1B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {121} \quad M_{A1B2} = {24.2}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {134.8} \quad n_{A1B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {174} \quad M_{A2B1} = {34.8}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {334.8} \quad n_{A2B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {208} \quad M_{A2B2} = {41.6}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {117.2} \quad n_{A2B2} = {5}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {20} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {19}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {20} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {16}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {28727} - \frac{729^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {28727} - \frac{531441}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {28727} - {26572.05}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2154.95}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {288.8 + 134.8 + 334.8 + 117.2}\)  \(SS_{within} = {875.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2154.95} - {875.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1279.35}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {347}  \quad n_{A_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {382}  \quad n_{A_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.53}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{347}^2}{10} + \frac{{382}^2}{10}} - \frac{{729}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{120409}}{10} + \frac{{145924}}{10}} - \frac{{531441}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{12040.9} + {14592.4}} - {26572.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {26633.3} - {26572.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {61.25}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{61.25}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {61.25}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{61.25}{54.72}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.12}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{61.25}{{2154.95} - {252.05} - {966.05}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{61.25}{936.85}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.07}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {1.12}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.07}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {400}  \quad n_{B_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {329}  \quad n_{B_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.53}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{400}^2}{10} + \frac{{329}^2}{10}} - \frac{{729}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{160000}}{10} + \frac{{108241}}{10}} - \frac{{531441}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{16000.0} + {10824.1}} - {26572.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {26824.1} - {26572.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {252.05}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{252.05}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {252.05}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{252.05}{54.72}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {4.61}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{252.05}{{2154.95} - {61.25} - {966.05}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{252.05}{1127.65}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.22}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {4.61}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.22}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.53}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1279.35} - {61.25} - {252.05}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {966.05}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{966.05}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {966.05}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{966.05}{54.72}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {17.65}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{966.05}{{2154.95} - {61.25} - {252.05}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{966.05}{1841.65}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.52}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {17.65}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.52}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 5
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {87} \quad M_{A1B1} = {14.5}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {9.5} \quad n_{A1B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {83} \quad M_{A1B2} = {13.83}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {22.83} \quad n_{A1B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {125} \quad M_{A1B3} = {20.83}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {22.83} \quad n_{A1B3} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {102} \quad M_{A2B1} = {17.0}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {20.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {102} \quad M_{A2B2} = {17.0}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {4.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {130} \quad M_{A2B3} = {21.67}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {19.33} \quad n_{A2B3} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {110} \quad M_{A3B1} = {18.33}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {25.33} \quad n_{A3B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {106} \quad M_{A3B2} = {17.67}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {19.33} \quad n_{A3B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {85} \quad M_{A3B3} = {14.17}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {50.83} \quad n_{A3B3} = {6}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {54} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {53}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {54} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {45}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16586} - \frac{930^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16586} - \frac{864900}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16586} - {16016.67}\)  \(SS_{total} = {569.33}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {9.5 + 22.83 + 22.83 + 20.0 + 4.0 + 19.33 + 25.33 + 19.33 + 50.83}\)  \(SS_{within} = {193.98}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {569.33} - {193.98}\)  \(SS_{between} = {375.35}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {295}  \quad n_{A_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {334}  \quad n_{A_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {301}  \quad n_{A_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{295}^2}{18} + \frac{{334}^2}{18} + \frac{{301}^2}{18}} - \frac{{930}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{87025}}{18} + \frac{{111556}}{18} + \frac{{90601}}{18}} - \frac{{864900}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{4834.72} + {6197.56} + {5033.39}} - {16016.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {16065.67} - {16016.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {49.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{49.0}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {24.5}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{24.5}{4.31}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {5.68}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{49.0}{{569.33} - {76.77} - {249.58}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{49.0}{242.98}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.2}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {45}) = {5.68}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.2}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {299}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {291}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {340}  \quad n_{B_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{299}^2}{18} + \frac{{291}^2}{18} + \frac{{340}^2}{18}} - \frac{{930}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{89401}}{18} + \frac{{84681}}{18} + \frac{{115600}}{18}} - \frac{{864900}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{4966.72} + {4704.5} + {6422.22}} - {16016.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {16093.44} - {16016.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {76.77}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{76.77}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {38.38}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{38.38}{4.31}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {8.9}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{76.77}{{569.33} - {49.0} - {249.58}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{76.77}{270.75}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.28}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {45}) = {8.9}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.28}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.77}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {375.35} - {49.0} - {76.77}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {249.58}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{249.58}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {62.4}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{62.4}{4.31}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {14.48}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{249.58}{{569.33} - {49.0} - {76.77}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{249.58}{443.56}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.56}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {45}) = {14.48}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.56}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 6
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {313} \quad M_{A1B1} = {26.08}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {272.92} \quad n_{A1B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {263} \quad M_{A1B2} = {21.92}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {116.92} \quad n_{A1B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {376} \quad M_{A2B1} = {31.33}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {560.67} \quad n_{A2B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {292} \quad M_{A2B2} = {24.33}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {556.67} \quad n_{A2B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {324} \quad M_{A3B1} = {27.0}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {444.0} \quad n_{A3B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {387} \quad M_{A3B2} = {32.25}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {562.25} \quad n_{A3B2} = {12}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {72} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {71}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {72} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {66}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {56557} - \frac{1955^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {56557} - \frac{3822025}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {56557} - {53083.68}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3473.32}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {272.92 + 116.92 + 560.67 + 556.67 + 444.0 + 562.25}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2513.43}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3473.32} - {2513.43}\)  \(SS_{between} = {959.89}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {576}  \quad n_{A_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {668}  \quad n_{A_2} = {24}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {711}  \quad n_{A_3} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.14}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{576}^2}{24} + \frac{{668}^2}{24} + \frac{{711}^2}{24}} - \frac{{1955}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{331776}}{24} + \frac{{446224}}{24} + \frac{{505521}}{24}} - \frac{{3822025}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{13824.0} + {18592.67} + {21063.38}} - {53083.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {53480.05} - {53083.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {396.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{396.37}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {198.18}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{198.18}{38.08}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {5.2}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{396.37}{{3473.32} - {70.01} - {493.51}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{396.37}{2909.8}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.14}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {5.2}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.14}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1013}  \quad n_{B_1} = {36}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {942}  \quad n_{B_2} = {36}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.99}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1013}^2}{36} + \frac{{942}^2}{36}} - \frac{{1955}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1026169}}{36} + \frac{{887364}}{36}} - \frac{{3822025}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{28504.69} + {24649.0}} - {53083.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {53153.69} - {53083.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {70.01}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{70.01}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {70.01}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{70.01}{38.08}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {1.84}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{70.01}{{3473.32} - {396.37} - {493.51}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{70.01}{2583.44}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {66}) = {1.84}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.14}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {959.89} - {396.37} - {70.01}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {493.51}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{493.51}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {246.76}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{246.76}{38.08}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {6.48}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{493.51}{{3473.32} - {396.37} - {70.01}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{493.51}{3006.94}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {6.48}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.16}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 7
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {132} \quad M_{A1B1} = {22.0}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {72.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {135} \quad M_{A1B2} = {22.5}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {27.5} \quad n_{A1B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {165} \quad M_{A2B1} = {27.5}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {225.5} \quad n_{A2B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {171} \quad M_{A2B2} = {28.5}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {47.5} \quad n_{A2B2} = {6}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {24} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {23}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {24} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {20}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15725} - \frac{603^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15725} - \frac{363609}{24}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15725} - {15150.38}\)  \(SS_{total} = {574.62}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {72.0 + 27.5 + 225.5 + 47.5}\)  \(SS_{within} = {372.5}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {574.62} - {372.5}\)  \(SS_{between} = {202.12}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {267}  \quad n_{A_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {336}  \quad n_{A_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{267}^2}{12} + \frac{{336}^2}{12}} - \frac{{603}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{71289}}{12} + \frac{{112896}}{12}} - \frac{{363609}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{5940.75} + {9408.0}} - {15150.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {15348.75} - {15150.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {198.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{198.37}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {198.37}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{198.37}{18.62}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {10.65}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{198.37}{{574.62} - {3.37} - {0.38}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{198.37}{570.87}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.35}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {10.65}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.35}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {297}  \quad n_{B_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {306}  \quad n_{B_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{297}^2}{12} + \frac{{306}^2}{12}} - \frac{{603}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{88209}}{12} + \frac{{93636}}{12}} - \frac{{363609}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{7350.75} + {7803.0}} - {15150.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {15153.75} - {15150.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {3.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{3.37}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {3.37}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{3.37}{18.62}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.18}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3.37}{{574.62} - {198.37} - {0.38}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3.37}{375.87}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {0.18}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {202.12} - {198.37} - {3.37}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {0.38}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{0.38}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {0.38}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{0.38}{18.62}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.02}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.38}{{574.62} - {198.37} - {3.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.38}{372.88}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {0.02}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 8
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {429} \quad M_{A1B1} = {61.29}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {91.43} \quad n_{A1B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {464} \quad M_{A1B2} = {66.29}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {427.43} \quad n_{A1B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {306} \quad M_{A1B3} = {43.71}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {319.43} \quad n_{A1B3} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {415} \quad M_{A2B1} = {59.29}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {225.43} \quad n_{A2B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {414} \quad M_{A2B2} = {59.14}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {194.86} \quad n_{A2B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {471} \quad M_{A2B3} = {67.29}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {467.43} \quad n_{A2B3} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {439} \quad M_{A3B1} = {62.71}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {131.43} \quad n_{A3B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {425} \quad M_{A3B2} = {60.71}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {297.43} \quad n_{A3B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {335} \quad M_{A3B3} = {47.86}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {320.86} \quad n_{A3B3} = {7}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {63} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {62}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {63} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {54}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {223048} - \frac{3698^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{total} = {223048} - \frac{13675204}{63}\)  \(SS_{total} = {223048} - {217066.73}\)  \(SS_{total} = {5981.27}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {91.43 + 427.43 + 319.43 + 225.43 + 194.86 + 467.43 + 131.43 + 297.43 + 320.86}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2475.73}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {5981.27} - {2475.73}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3505.54}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1199}  \quad n_{A_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1300}  \quad n_{A_2} = {21}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {1199}  \quad n_{A_3} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.17}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1199}^2}{21} + \frac{{1300}^2}{21} + \frac{{1199}^2}{21}} - \frac{{3698}^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1437601}}{21} + \frac{{1690000}}{21} + \frac{{1437601}}{21}} - \frac{{13675204}}{63}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{68457.19} + {80476.19} + {68457.19}} - {217066.73}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {217390.57} - {217066.73}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {323.84}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{323.84}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {161.92}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{161.92}{45.85}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {3.53}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{323.84}{{5981.27} - {1049.56} - {2132.14}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{323.84}{2799.57}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.12}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {3.53}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.12}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1283}  \quad n_{B_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1303}  \quad n_{B_2} = {21}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {1112}  \quad n_{B_3} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.17}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1283}^2}{21} + \frac{{1303}^2}{21} + \frac{{1112}^2}{21}} - \frac{{3698}^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1646089}}{21} + \frac{{1697809}}{21} + \frac{{1236544}}{21}} - \frac{{13675204}}{63}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{78385.19} + {80848.05} + {58883.05}} - {217066.73}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {218116.29} - {217066.73}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {1049.56}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{1049.56}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {524.78}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{524.78}{45.85}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {11.45}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1049.56}{{5981.27} - {323.84} - {2132.14}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1049.56}{3525.29}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.3}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {11.45}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.3}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {181508} - \frac{3528^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {181508} - \frac{12446784}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {181508} - {172872.0}\)  \(SS_{total} = {8636.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {345.88 + 531.88 + 671.5 + 1095.88 + 166.88 + 407.88 + 379.5 + 490.88 + 754.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {4844.28}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {8636.0} - {4844.28}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3791.72}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{969.3}{76.89}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {12.61}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{596.38}{76.89}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {7.76}\)

 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.52}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {3791.72} - {1938.59} - {1192.75}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {660.38}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{660.38}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {165.1}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{165.1}{76.89}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.15}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{660.38}{{8636.0} - {1938.59} - {1192.75}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{660.38}{5504.66}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.12}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({4}, {63}) = {2.15}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.12}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1900.33}{{7382.22} - {2971.26} - {1041.1}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1900.33}{3369.86}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.56}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {72}) = {93.11}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.56}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.91}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {5912.69} - {2971.26} - {1900.33}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1041.1}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1041.1}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {520.55}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1041.1}{{7382.22} - {2971.26} - {1900.33}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1041.1}{2510.63}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.41}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {72}) = {25.5}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.41}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.19}{{4796.31} - {1003.87} - {1365.85}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.19}{2426.59}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {42}) = {0.16}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {89297} - \frac{1611^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {89297} - \frac{2595321}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {89297} - {86510.7}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2786.3}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {104.0 + 84.8 + 428.8 + 208.8 + 371.2 + 246.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1444.4}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2786.3} - {1444.4}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1341.9}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{348.4}{60.18}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {5.79}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{696.8}{{2786.3} - {61.64} - {583.46}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{696.8}{2141.2}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.33}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {5.79}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.33}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {827}  \quad n_{B_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {784}  \quad n_{B_2} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{827}^2}{15} + \frac{{784}^2}{15}} - \frac{{1611}^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{683929}}{15} + \frac{{614656}}{15}} - \frac{{2595321}}{30}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{45595.27} + {40977.07}} - {86510.7}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {86572.34} - {86510.7}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {61.64}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{61.64}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {61.64}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{61.64}{{2786.3} - {696.8} - {583.46}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{61.64}{1506.04}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {1.02}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.04}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.4}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1341.9} - {696.8} - {61.64}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {583.46}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{583.46}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {291.73}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{291.73}{60.18}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.85}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{583.46}{{2786.3} - {696.8} - {61.64}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{583.46}{2027.86}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {4.85}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.29}\)
