 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{160.0}{{4308.0} - {1795.6} - {490.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{160.0}{2022.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {3.09}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {796}  \quad n_{B_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1064}  \quad n_{B_2} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.4}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{796}^2}{20} + \frac{{1064}^2}{20}} - \frac{{1860}^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{633616}}{20} + \frac{{1132096}}{20}} - \frac{{3459600}}{40}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{31680.8} + {56604.8}} - {86490.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {88285.6} - {86490.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {1795.6}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{1795.6}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {1795.6}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{1795.6}{51.73}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {34.71}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1795.6}{{4308.0} - {160.0} - {490.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1795.6}{3658.0}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.49}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {34.71}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.49}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.4}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2445.6} - {160.0} - {1795.6}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {490.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{490.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {490.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{490.0}{51.73}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {9.47}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{490.0}{{4308.0} - {160.0} - {1795.6}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{490.0}{2352.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.21}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {9.47}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.21}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 20
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {974} \quad M_{A1B1} = {69.57}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {3561.43} \quad n_{A1B1} = {14}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {987} \quad M_{A1B2} = {70.5}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {3463.5} \quad n_{A1B2} = {14}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {842} \quad M_{A1B3} = {60.14}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {2711.71} \quad n_{A1B3} = {14}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {794} \quad M_{A2B1} = {56.71}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {3278.86} \quad n_{A2B1} = {14}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {832} \quad M_{A2B2} = {59.43}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {957.43} \quad n_{A2B2} = {14}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {554} \quad M_{A2B3} = {39.57}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {1537.43} \quad n_{A2B3} = {14}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {726} \quad M_{A3B1} = {51.86}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {1951.71} \quad n_{A3B1} = {14}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {719} \quad M_{A3B2} = {51.36}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {3729.21} \quad n_{A3B2} = {14}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {936} \quad M_{A3B3} = {66.86}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {3483.71} \quad n_{A3B3} = {14}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {126} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {125}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {126} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {117}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {466212} - \frac{7364^2}{126}\)  \(SS_{total} = {466212} - \frac{54228496}{126}\)  \(SS_{total} = {466212} - {430384.89}\)  \(SS_{total} = {35827.11}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {3561.43 + 3463.5 + 2711.71 + 3278.86 + 957.43 + 1537.43 + 1951.71 + 3729.21 + 3483.71}\)  \(SS_{within} = {24674.99}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {35827.11} - {24674.99}\)  \(SS_{between} = {11152.12}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {2803}  \quad n_{A_1} = {42}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {2180}  \quad n_{A_2} = {42}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {2381}  \quad n_{A_3} = {42}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.07}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2803}^2}{42} + \frac{{2180}^2}{42} + \frac{{2381}^2}{42}} - \frac{{7364}^2}{126}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{7856809}}{42} + \frac{{4752400}}{42} + \frac{{5669161}}{42}} - \frac{{54228496}}{126}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{187066.88} + {113152.38} + {134980.02}} - {430384.89}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {435199.28} - {430384.89}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {4814.39}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{4814.39}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {2407.2}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{2407.2}{210.9}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {11.41}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{4814.39}{{35827.11} - {560.44} - {5777.29}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{4814.39}{29489.38}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {117}) = {11.41}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {2494}  \quad n_{B_1} = {42}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {2538}  \quad n_{B_2} = {42}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {2332}  \quad n_{B_3} = {42}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.07}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2494}^2}{42} + \frac{{2538}^2}{42} + \frac{{2332}^2}{42}} - \frac{{7364}^2}{126}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{6220036}}{42} + \frac{{6441444}}{42} + \frac{{5438224}}{42}} - \frac{{54228496}}{126}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{148096.1} + {153367.71} + {129481.52}} - {430384.89}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {430945.33} - {430384.89}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {560.44}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{560.44}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {280.22}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{280.22}{210.9}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {1.33}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{560.44}{{35827.11} - {4814.39} - {5777.29}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{560.44}{25235.43}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {117}) = {1.33}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.45}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {11152.12} - {4814.39} - {560.44}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {5777.29}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{5777.29}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {1444.32}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{1444.32}{210.9}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {6.85}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5777.29}{{35827.11} - {4814.39} - {560.44}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5777.29}{30452.28}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.19}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {117}) = {6.85}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.19}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 21
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {464} \quad M_{A1B1} = {35.69}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {1252.77} \quad n_{A1B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {459} \quad M_{A1B2} = {35.31}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {1196.77} \quad n_{A1B2} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {544} \quad M_{A2B1} = {41.85}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {3223.69} \quad n_{A2B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {557} \quad M_{A2B2} = {42.85}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {1447.69} \quad n_{A2B2} = {13}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {52} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {51}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {52} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {48}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {86518} - \frac{2024^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {86518} - \frac{4096576}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {86518} - {78780.31}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7737.69}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1252.77 + 1196.77 + 3223.69 + 1447.69}\)  \(SS_{within} = {7120.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {7737.69} - {7120.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = {616.77}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {923}  \quad n_{A_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1101}  \quad n_{A_2} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{923}^2}{26} + \frac{{1101}^2}{26}} - \frac{{2024}^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{851929}}{26} + \frac{{1212201}}{26}} - \frac{{4096576}}{52}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{32766.5} + {46623.12}} - {78780.31}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {79389.62} - {78780.31}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {609.31}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{609.31}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {609.31}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{609.31}{148.35}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {4.11}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{609.31}{{7737.69} - {1.22} - {6.24}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{609.31}{7730.23}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {4.11}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1008}  \quad n_{B_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1016}  \quad n_{B_2} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1008}^2}{26} + \frac{{1016}^2}{26}} - \frac{{2024}^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1016064}}{26} + \frac{{1032256}}{26}} - \frac{{4096576}}{52}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{39079.38} + {39702.15}} - {78780.31}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {78781.53} - {78780.31}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {1.22}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{1.22}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {1.22}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{1.22}{148.35}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.01}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1.22}{{7737.69} - {609.31} - {6.24}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1.22}{7122.14}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {0.01}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {616.77} - {609.31} - {1.22}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {6.24}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{6.24}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {6.24}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{6.24}{148.35}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.04}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{6.24}{{7737.69} - {609.31} - {1.22}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{6.24}{7127.16}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {0.04}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 22
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {121} \quad M_{A1B1} = {13.44}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {66.22} \quad n_{A1B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {126} \quad M_{A1B2} = {14.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {68.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {188} \quad M_{A2B1} = {20.89}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {32.89} \quad n_{A2B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {171} \quad M_{A2B2} = {19.0}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {126.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {177} \quad M_{A3B1} = {19.67}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {84.0} \quad n_{A3B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {145} \quad M_{A3B2} = {16.11}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {50.89} \quad n_{A3B2} = {9}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {54} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {53}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {54} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {48}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16812} - \frac{928^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16812} - \frac{861184}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16812} - {15947.85}\)  \(SS_{total} = {864.15}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {66.22 + 68.0 + 32.89 + 126.0 + 84.0 + 50.89}\)  \(SS_{within} = {428.0}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {864.15} - {428.0}\)  \(SS_{between} = {436.15}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {247}  \quad n_{A_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {359}  \quad n_{A_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {322}  \quad n_{A_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.19}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{247}^2}{18} + \frac{{359}^2}{18} + \frac{{322}^2}{18}} - \frac{{928}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{61009}}{18} + \frac{{128881}}{18} + \frac{{103684}}{18}} - \frac{{861184}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{3389.39} + {7160.06} + {5760.22}} - {15947.85}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {16309.67} - {15947.85}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {361.82}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{361.82}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {180.91}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{180.91}{8.92}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {20.28}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{361.82}{{864.15} - {35.85} - {38.48}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{361.82}{789.82}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.46}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {20.28}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.46}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {486}  \quad n_{B_1} = {27}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {442}  \quad n_{B_2} = {27}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{486}^2}{27} + \frac{{442}^2}{27}} - \frac{{928}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{236196}}{27} + \frac{{195364}}{27}} - \frac{{861184}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{8748.0} + {7235.7}} - {15947.85}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {15983.7} - {15947.85}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {35.85}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{35.85}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {35.85}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{35.85}{8.92}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {4.02}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{35.85}{{864.15} - {361.82} - {38.48}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{35.85}{463.85}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {4.02}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.08}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.19}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {436.15} - {361.82} - {35.85}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {38.48}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{38.48}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {19.24}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{19.24}{8.92}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.16}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{38.48}{{864.15} - {361.82} - {35.85}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{38.48}{466.48}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {2.16}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.08}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 23
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {405} \quad M_{A1B1} = {40.5}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {230.5} \quad n_{A1B1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {353} \quad M_{A1B2} = {35.3}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {162.1} \quad n_{A1B2} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {410} \quad M_{A2B1} = {41.0}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {96.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {288} \quad M_{A2B2} = {28.8}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {119.6} \quad n_{A2B2} = {10}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {40} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {39}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {40} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {36}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {54576} - \frac{1456^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{total} = {54576} - \frac{2119936}{40}\)  \(SS_{total} = {54576} - {52998.4}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1577.6}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {230.5 + 162.1 + 96.0 + 119.6}\)  \(SS_{within} = {608.2}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1577.6} - {608.2}\)  \(SS_{between} = {969.4}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {758}  \quad n_{A_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {698}  \quad n_{A_2} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{758}^2}{20} + \frac{{698}^2}{20}} - \frac{{1456}^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{574564}}{20} + \frac{{487204}}{20}} - \frac{{2119936}}{40}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{28728.2} + {24360.2}} - {52998.4}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {53088.4} - {52998.4}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {90.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{90.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {90.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{90.0}{16.89}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {5.33}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{90.0}{{1577.6} - {756.9} - {122.5}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{90.0}{698.2}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.13}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {5.33}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.13}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {815}  \quad n_{B_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {641}  \quad n_{B_2} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{815}^2}{20} + \frac{{641}^2}{20}} - \frac{{1456}^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{664225}}{20} + \frac{{410881}}{20}} - \frac{{2119936}}{40}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{33211.25} + {20544.05}} - {52998.4}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {53755.3} - {52998.4}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {756.9}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{756.9}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {756.9}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{756.9}{16.89}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {44.81}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{756.9}{{1577.6} - {90.0} - {122.5}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{756.9}{1365.1}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.55}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {44.81}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.55}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {969.4} - {90.0} - {756.9}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {122.5}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{122.5}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {122.5}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{122.5}{16.89}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {7.25}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{122.5}{{1577.6} - {90.0} - {756.9}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{122.5}{730.7}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {7.25}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.17}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 24
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {246} \quad M_{A1B1} = {35.14}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {112.86} \quad n_{A1B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {321} \quad M_{A1B2} = {45.86}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {92.86} \quad n_{A1B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {283} \quad M_{A1B3} = {40.43}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {345.71} \quad n_{A1B3} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {270} \quad M_{A2B1} = {38.57}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {101.71} \quad n_{A2B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {345} \quad M_{A2B2} = {49.29}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {95.43} \quad n_{A2B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {246} \quad M_{A2B3} = {35.14}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {76.86} \quad n_{A2B3} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {272} \quad M_{A3B1} = {38.86}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {320.86} \quad n_{A3B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {300} \quad M_{A3B2} = {42.86}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {272.86} \quad n_{A3B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {287} \quad M_{A3B3} = {41.0}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {266.0} \quad n_{A3B3} = {7}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {63} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {62}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {63} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {54}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {107748} - \frac{2570^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{total} = {107748} - \frac{6604900}{63}\)  \(SS_{total} = {107748} - {104839.68}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2908.32}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {112.86 + 92.86 + 345.71 + 101.71 + 95.43 + 76.86 + 320.86 + 272.86 + 266.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1685.15}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2908.32} - {1685.15}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1223.17}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {850}  \quad n_{A_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {861}  \quad n_{A_2} = {21}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {859}  \quad n_{A_3} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.17}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{850}^2}{21} + \frac{{861}^2}{21} + \frac{{859}^2}{21}} - \frac{{2570}^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{722500}}{21} + \frac{{741321}}{21} + \frac{{737881}}{21}} - \frac{{6604900}}{63}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{34404.76} + {35301.0} + {35137.19}} - {104839.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {104842.95} - {104839.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {3.27}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{3.27}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1.64}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1.64}{31.21}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.05}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3.27}{{2908.32} - {872.51} - {347.39}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3.27}{1688.42}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {0.05}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {788}  \quad n_{B_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {966}  \quad n_{B_2} = {21}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {816}  \quad n_{B_3} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.17}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{788}^2}{21} + \frac{{966}^2}{21} + \frac{{816}^2}{21}} - \frac{{2570}^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{620944}}{21} + \frac{{933156}}{21} + \frac{{665856}}{21}} - \frac{{6604900}}{63}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{29568.76} + {44436.0} + {31707.43}} - {104839.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {105712.19} - {104839.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {872.51}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{872.51}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {436.26}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{436.26}{31.21}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {13.98}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{872.51}{{2908.32} - {3.27} - {347.39}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{872.51}{2557.66}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.34}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {13.98}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.34}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.54}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1223.17} - {3.27} - {872.51}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {347.39}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{347.39}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {86.85}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{86.85}{31.21}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.78}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{347.39}{{2908.32} - {3.27} - {872.51}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{347.39}{2032.54}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {54}) = {2.78}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.17}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {22309} - \frac{855^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {22309} - \frac{731025}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {22309} - {20306.25}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2002.75}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {349.56 + 110.22 + 192.89 + 284.22}\)  \(SS_{within} = {936.89}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2002.75} - {936.89}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1065.86}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {417}  \quad n_{A_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {438}  \quad n_{A_2} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{417}^2}{18} + \frac{{438}^2}{18}} - \frac{{855}^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{173889}}{18} + \frac{{191844}}{18}} - \frac{{731025}}{36}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{9660.5} + {10658.0}} - {20306.25}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {20318.5} - {20306.25}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {12.25}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{12.25}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {12.25}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{12.25}{29.28}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.42}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{12.25}{{2002.75} - {633.37} - {420.24}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{12.25}{949.14}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {32}) = {0.42}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {503}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {352}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{503}^2}{18} + \frac{{352}^2}{18}} - \frac{{855}^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{253009}}{18} + \frac{{123904}}{18}} - \frac{{731025}}{36}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{14056.06} + {6883.56}} - {20306.25}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {20939.62} - {20306.25}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {633.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{633.37}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {633.37}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{633.37}{29.28}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {21.63}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{633.37}{{2002.75} - {12.25} - {420.24}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{633.37}{1570.26}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.4}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {32}) = {21.63}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.4}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1065.86} - {12.25} - {633.37}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {420.24}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{420.24}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {420.24}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{420.24}{29.28}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {14.35}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{420.24}{{2002.75} - {12.25} - {633.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{420.24}{1357.13}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {32}) = {14.35}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {129712} - \frac{3324^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {129712} - \frac{11048976}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {129712} - {122766.4}\)  \(SS_{total} = {6945.6}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {873.73 + 378.4 + 277.73 + 999.73 + 1158.93 + 452.4}\)  \(SS_{within} = {4140.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {6945.6} - {4140.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2804.68}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1100}  \quad n_{A_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1050}  \quad n_{A_2} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {1174}  \quad n_{A_3} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.87}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1100}^2}{30} + \frac{{1050}^2}{30} + \frac{{1174}^2}{30}} - \frac{{3324}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1210000}}{30} + \frac{{1102500}}{30} + \frac{{1378276}}{30}} - \frac{{11048976}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{40333.33} + {36750.0} + {45942.53}} - {122766.4}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {123025.86} - {122766.4}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {259.46}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{259.46}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {129.73}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{129.73}{49.3}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {2.63}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{259.46}{{6945.6} - {106.71} - {2438.51}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{259.46}{4400.38}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {2.63}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1711}  \quad n_{B_1} = {45}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1613}  \quad n_{B_2} = {45}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {6.95}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1711}^2}{45} + \frac{{1613}^2}{45}} - \frac{{3324}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2927521}}{45} + \frac{{2601769}}{45}} - \frac{{11048976}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{65056.02} + {57817.09}} - {122766.4}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {122873.11} - {122766.4}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {106.71}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{106.71}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {106.71}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{106.71}{49.3}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {2.16}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{106.71}{{6945.6} - {259.46} - {2438.51}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{106.71}{4247.63}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {84}) = {2.16}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.87}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2804.68} - {259.46} - {106.71}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {2438.51}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{2438.51}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {1219.26}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{1219.26}{49.3}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {24.73}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2438.51}{{6945.6} - {259.46} - {106.71}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2438.51}{6579.43}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.37}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {24.73}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.37}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1001.72}{142.23}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {7.04}\)

Problem 30
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {223} \quad M_{A1B1} = {37.17}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {88.83} \quad n_{A1B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {185} \quad M_{A1B2} = {30.83}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {74.83} \quad n_{A1B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {175} \quad M_{A1B3} = {29.17}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {38.83} \quad n_{A1B3} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {182} \quad M_{A2B1} = {30.33}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {87.33} \quad n_{A2B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {201} \quad M_{A2B2} = {33.5}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {27.5} \quad n_{A2B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {154} \quad M_{A2B3} = {25.67}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {51.33} \quad n_{A2B3} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {225} \quad M_{A3B1} = {37.5}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {121.5} \quad n_{A3B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {224} \quad M_{A3B2} = {37.33}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {107.33} \quad n_{A3B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {156} \quad M_{A3B3} = {26.0}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {154.0} \quad n_{A3B3} = {6}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {54} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {53}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {54} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {45}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {56911} - \frac{1725^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {56911} - \frac{2975625}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {56911} - {55104.17}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1806.83}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {88.83 + 74.83 + 38.83 + 87.33 + 27.5 + 51.33 + 121.5 + 107.33 + 154.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {751.48}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1806.83} - {751.48}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1055.35}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {583}  \quad n_{A_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {537}  \quad n_{A_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {605}  \quad n_{A_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{583}^2}{18} + \frac{{537}^2}{18} + \frac{{605}^2}{18}} - \frac{{1725}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{339889}}{18} + \frac{{288369}}{18} + \frac{{366025}}{18}} - \frac{{2975625}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{18882.72} + {16020.5} + {20334.72}} - {55104.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {55237.94} - {55104.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {133.77}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{133.77}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {66.89}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{66.89}{16.7}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {4.01}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{133.77}{{1806.83} - {686.11} - {235.47}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{133.77}{885.25}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.15}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {45}) = {4.01}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.15}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {630}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {610}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {485}  \quad n_{B_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{630}^2}{18} + \frac{{610}^2}{18} + \frac{{485}^2}{18}} - \frac{{1725}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{396900}}{18} + \frac{{372100}}{18} + \frac{{235225}}{18}} - \frac{{2975625}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{22050.0} + {20672.22} + {13068.06}} - {55104.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {55790.28} - {55104.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {686.11}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{686.11}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {343.06}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{343.06}{16.7}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {20.54}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{686.11}{{1806.83} - {133.77} - {235.47}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{686.11}{1437.59}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.48}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {45}) = {20.54}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.48}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.58}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1055.35} - {133.77} - {686.11}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {235.47}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{235.47}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {58.87}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{58.87}{16.7}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {3.53}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{235.47}{{1806.83} - {133.77} - {686.11}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{235.47}{986.95}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.24}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {45}) = {3.53}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.24}\)

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {1302} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {30890} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {56}\) 
\(T_{A} = {313} \quad SS_{A} = {31.21}\)  \(T_{B} = {309} \quad SS_{B} = {86.93}\)  \(T_{C} = {288} \quad SS_{C} = {7.43}\)  \(T_{D} = {392} \quad SS_{D} = {46.0}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.78}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {56} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {55}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {56} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {52}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {30890} - \frac{1302^2}{56}\]  \[SS_{total} = {30890} - \frac{1695204}{56}\]  \[SS_{total} = {30890} - {30271.5}\]  \[SS_{total} = {618.5}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {31.21 + 86.93 + 7.43 + 46.0}\]  \[SS_{within} = {171.57}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {618.5} - {171.57}\]  \[SS_{between} = {446.93}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{446.93}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {148.98}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{171.57}{52}\]  \[MS_{within} = {3.3}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{148.98}{3.3}\]  \[F_{obt} = {45.15}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{446.93}{618.5}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.72}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {52}) = {45.15}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.72}\)

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {36} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {35}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {36} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {32}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {8198} - \frac{532^2}{36}\]  \[SS_{total} = {8198} - \frac{283024}{36}\]  \[SS_{total} = {8198} - {7861.78}\]  \[SS_{total} = {336.22}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {89.56 + 56.0 + 80.0 + 78.0}\]  \[SS_{within} = {303.56}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {336.22} - {303.56}\]  \[SS_{between} = {32.66}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]

Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{10.89}{9.49}\]  \[F_{obt} = {1.15}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{32.66}{336.22}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.1}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({3}, {32}) = {1.15}, p > {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.1}\)

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {39} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {38}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {2}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {39} - {3}\]  \[df_{within} = {36}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {13081} - \frac{709^2}{39}\]  \[SS_{total} = {13081} - \frac{502681}{39}\]  \[SS_{total} = {13081} - {12889.26}\]  \[SS_{total} = {191.74}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {68.77 + 48.0 + 65.69}\]  \[SS_{within} = {182.46}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {191.74} - {182.46}\]  \[SS_{between} = {9.28}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]

Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18317} - \frac{941^2}{52}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18317} - \frac{885481}{52}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18317} - {17028.48}\]  \[SS_{total} = {1288.52}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {236.77 + 322.92 + 227.69 + 157.23}\]  \[SS_{within} = {944.61}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1288.52} - {944.61}\]  \[SS_{between} = {343.91}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{343.91}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {114.64}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{944.61}{48}\]  \[MS_{within} = {19.68}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{114.64}{19.68}\]  \[F_{obt} = {5.83}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{343.91}{1288.52}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.27}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {48}) = {5.83}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.27}\)

The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({3}, {24}) = {0.43}, p > {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.05}\)

\(T_{A} = {427} \quad SS_{A} = {471.73}\)  \(T_{B} = {646} \quad SS_{B} = {168.93}\)  \(T_{C} = {443} \quad SS_{C} = {263.73}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.15}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {45} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {44}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {2}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {45} - {3}\]  \[df_{within} = {42}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {53964} - \frac{1516^2}{45}\]  \[SS_{total} = {53964} - \frac{2298256}{45}\]  \[SS_{total} = {53964} - {51072.36}\]  \[SS_{total} = {2891.64}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {471.73 + 168.93 + 263.73}\]  \[SS_{within} = {904.39}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {2891.64} - {904.39}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1987.25}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{1987.25}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {993.62}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{904.39}{42}\]  \[MS_{within} = {21.53}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{993.62}{21.53}\]  \[F_{obt} = {46.15}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{1987.25}{2891.64}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.69}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {46.15}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.69}\)

 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {20.2}, M_B = {21.27}\] \[SS_A = {124.4}, SS_B = {166.93}\] \[n_A = {15}, n_B = {15}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.76}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {28}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{124.4 + 166.93}{14 + 14}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{291.33}{28}\]  \[s_p^2 = {10.4}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{10.4}{15} + \frac{10.4}{15}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{0.69 + 0.69}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{1.38}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {1.17}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(20.2 - 21.27) - {0}}{1.17}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-1.07}{1.17}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-0.91}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(20.2 - 21.27)}{{{\sqrt{10.4}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-1.07)}{3.22}\]  \[d = {-0.33}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(28) = -0.91, p > 0.01, d = -0.33

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(39.0 - 38.56) - {0}}{1.38}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{0.44}{1.38}\]  \[t_{obt} = {0.32}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(39.0 - 38.56)}{{{\sqrt{8.51}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(0.44)}{2.92}\]  \[d = {0.15}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(16) = 0.32, p > 0.01, d = 0.15

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(31.33 - 21.0) - {0}}{2.17}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{10.33}{2.17}\]  \[t_{obt} = {4.76}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(31.33 - 21.0)}{{{\sqrt{28.3}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(10.33)}{5.32}\]  \[d = {1.94}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(22) = 4.76, p < 0.01, d = 1.94

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(54.86 - 43.29) - {0}}{2.17}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{11.57}{2.17}\]  \[t_{obt} = {5.33}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(54.86 - 43.29)}{{{\sqrt{32.87}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(11.57)}{5.73}\]  \[d = {2.02}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(26) = 5.33, p < 0.05, d = 2.02

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {1037} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {18563} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {60}\) 
\(T_{A} = {245} \quad SS_{A} = {57.33}\)  \(T_{B} = {256} \quad SS_{B} = {100.93}\)  \(T_{C} = {217} \quad SS_{C} = {79.73}\)  \(T_{D} = {319} \quad SS_{D} = {30.93}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {60} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {59}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {60} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {56}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18563} - \frac{1037^2}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18563} - \frac{1075369}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18563} - {17922.82}\]  \[SS_{total} = {640.18}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {57.33 + 100.93 + 79.73 + 30.93}\]  \[SS_{within} = {268.92}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {640.18} - {268.92}\]  \[SS_{between} = {371.26}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{371.26}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {123.75}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{268.92}{56}\]  \[MS_{within} = {4.8}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{123.75}{4.8}\]  \[F_{obt} = {25.78}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{371.26}{640.18}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.58}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {56}) = {25.78}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.58}\)

Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {382} \quad M_{A1B1} = {31.83}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {293.67} \quad n_{A1B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {302} \quad M_{A1B2} = {25.17}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {227.67} \quad n_{A1B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {459} \quad M_{A2B1} = {38.25}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {120.25} \quad n_{A2B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {295} \quad M_{A2B2} = {24.58}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {324.92} \quad n_{A2B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {432} \quad M_{A3B1} = {36.0}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {148.0} \quad n_{A3B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {369} \quad M_{A3B2} = {30.75}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {202.25} \quad n_{A3B2} = {12}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {72} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {71}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {72} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {66}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {72785} - \frac{2239^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {72785} - \frac{5013121}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {72785} - {69626.68}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3158.32}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {293.67 + 227.67 + 120.25 + 324.92 + 148.0 + 202.25}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1316.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3158.32} - {1316.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1841.56}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {684}  \quad n_{A_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {754}  \quad n_{A_2} = {24}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {801}  \quad n_{A_3} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.94}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{684}^2}{24} + \frac{{754}^2}{24} + \frac{{801}^2}{24}} - \frac{{2239}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{467856}}{24} + \frac{{568516}}{24} + \frac{{641601}}{24}} - \frac{{5013121}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{19494.0} + {23688.17} + {26733.38}} - {69626.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {69915.55} - {69626.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {288.87}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{288.87}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {144.44}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{144.44}{19.95}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {7.24}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{288.87}{{3158.32} - {1309.01} - {243.68}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{288.87}{1605.63}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.18}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {7.24}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.18}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1273}  \quad n_{B_1} = {36}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {966}  \quad n_{B_2} = {36}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.04}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1273}^2}{36} + \frac{{966}^2}{36}} - \frac{{2239}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1620529}}{36} + \frac{{933156}}{36}} - \frac{{5013121}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{45014.69} + {25921.0}} - {69626.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {70935.69} - {69626.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {1309.01}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{1309.01}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {1309.01}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{1309.01}{19.95}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {65.61}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1309.01}{{3158.32} - {288.87} - {243.68}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1309.01}{2625.77}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.5}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {66}) = {65.61}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.5}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.94}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1841.56} - {288.87} - {1309.01}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {243.68}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{243.68}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {121.84}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{121.84}{19.95}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {6.11}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{243.68}{{3158.32} - {288.87} - {1309.01}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{243.68}{1560.44}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {6.11}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.16}\)

The necessary summary statistics for these data \[M_A = {14.4}\] \[{\sigma} = {4}\] \[n = {15}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 15\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 15\] 
The decision criteria:  \(z_{crit} = \pm{2.58}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}\) Calculate the standard error  \[\sigma_M = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{N}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{4}{\sqrt{15}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{4}{3.87}\]  \[\sigma_M = {1.03}\]  Calculate \(z_{obt}\)  \[z_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{\sigma_M}}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{14.4 - 15}{1.03}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{-0.6}{1.03}\]  \[z_{obt} = {-0.58}\]  Calculate Cohen’s d for effect size  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{\sigma}\]  \[d = \frac{14.4 - 15}{4}\]  \[d = \frac{-0.6}{4}\]  \[d = {-0.15}\]  The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  z = -0.58, p > 0.01, d = -0.15

New Summary statistics for the difference scores: \[M_D = {-6.18}, \quad \Sigma{D} = {-68}, \quad \Sigma{D^2} = {714}\] 
Calculate SS of the difference scores  \[SS_D = \Sigma D^2 - \frac{(\Sigma D)^2}{n}\]  \[SS_D = {714} - \frac{4624}{11}\]  \[SS_D = {714} - {420.36}\]  \[SS_D = {293.64}\] 
Calculate the variance of the difference scores  \[s_D^2 = \frac{SS_D}{df}\]  \[s_D^2 = \frac{293.64}{10}\]  \[s_D^2 = {29.36}\] 
Calculate the estimated standard error of the difference scores  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{29.36}{11}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{2.67}\]  \[s_{M_D} = {1.63}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-6.18 - 0}{1.63}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-6.18}{1.63}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-3.79}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{-6.18}{{{\sqrt{29.36}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{-6.18}{5.42}\]  \[d = {-1.14}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(10) = -3.79, p < 0.01, d = -1.14

New Summary statistics for the difference scores: \[M_D = {-6.21}, \quad \Sigma{D} = {-87}, \quad \Sigma{D^2} = {1279}\] 
Calculate SS of the difference scores  \[SS_D = \Sigma D^2 - \frac{(\Sigma D)^2}{n}\]  \[SS_D = {1279} - \frac{7569}{14}\]  \[SS_D = {1279} - {540.64}\]  \[SS_D = {738.36}\] 
Calculate the variance of the difference scores  \[s_D^2 = \frac{SS_D}{df}\]  \[s_D^2 = \frac{738.36}{13}\]  \[s_D^2 = {56.8}\] 
Calculate the estimated standard error of the difference scores  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{56.8}{14}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{4.06}\]  \[s_{M_D} = {2.01}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-6.21 - 0}{2.01}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-6.21}{2.01}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-3.09}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{-6.21}{{{\sqrt{56.8}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{-6.21}{7.54}\]  \[d = {-0.82}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(13) = -3.09, p < 0.01, d = -0.82

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {470} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {5696} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {40}\) 
\(T_{A} = {98} \quad SS_{A} = {39.5}\)  \(T_{B} = {98} \quad SS_{B} = {55.5}\)  \(T_{C} = {95} \quad SS_{C} = {18.88}\)  \(T_{D} = {86} \quad SS_{D} = {19.5}\)  \(T_{E} = {93} \quad SS_{E} = {27.88}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.71}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {40} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {39}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {40} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {35}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {5696} - \frac{470^2}{40}\]  \[SS_{total} = {5696} - \frac{220900}{40}\]  \[SS_{total} = {5696} - {5522.5}\]  \[SS_{total} = {173.5}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {39.5 + 55.5 + 18.88 + 19.5 + 27.88}\]  \[SS_{within} = {161.26}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {173.5} - {161.26}\]  \[SS_{between} = {12.24}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {8} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {7}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {35} - {7}\]  \[df_{error} = {28}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{52^2}{5} + \frac{58^2}{5} + \frac{55^2}{5} + \frac{67^2}{5} + \frac{64^2}{5} + \frac{62^2}{5} + \frac{54^2}{5} + \frac{58^2}{5}} - \frac{470^2}{40}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{2704}{5} + \frac{3364}{5} + \frac{3025}{5} + \frac{4489}{5} + \frac{4096}{5} + \frac{3844}{5} + \frac{2916}{5} + \frac{3364}{5}} - \frac{220900}{40}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{540.8} + {672.8} + {605.0} + {897.8} + {819.2} + {768.8} + {583.2} + {672.8}} - {5522.5}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {5560.4} - {5522.5}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {37.9}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {161.26} - {37.9}\]  \[SS_{error} = {123.36}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{12.24}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {3.06}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{123.36}{28}\]  \[MS_{error} = {4.41}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{3.06}{4.41}\]  \[F_{obt} = {0.69}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{12.24}{{173.5} - {37.9}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{12.24}{135.6}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({4}, {28}) = {0.69}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18350} - \frac{832^2}{39}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18350} - \frac{692224}{39}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18350} - {17749.33}\]  \[SS_{total} = {600.67}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {52.92 + 53.08 + 26.77}\]  \[SS_{within} = {132.77}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {600.67} - {132.77}\]  \[SS_{between} = {467.9}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {13} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {12}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {36} - {12}\]  \[df_{error} = {24}\]

Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{133.36}{{288.96} - {18.16}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{133.36}{270.8}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.49}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({4}, {16}) = {3.88}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.49}\)
